title: Hematoxylin and Eosin Stain of FFPE Tumor Tissue
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fh6p0","text":"This protocol describes the steps for doing a manual Hematoxylin and Eosin (H\u0026E) Stain of formalin fixed tissue. This stain is used to distinguish tumor tissue, background normal and stromal tissue, as well as tumor infiltrating immune cells. All pathology reviews of tumor tissue received from study participants is performed by examining the H\u0026E stained tissue.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"dmfcu","text":"This protocol is for FFPE tissues only. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9r415","text":"Staining with Hematoxylin and Eosin can vary over time and staining length may need to be empirically determined. If staining becomes too faint or uneven, new reagents will need to be substituted.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"335ls","text":"It is important to use tap water for steps 9, 11, and 13, as minerals dissolved in normal tap water contribute to the overall character of the stain.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"bg59i","text":"Make sure all de-paraffinization reagents (xylene and Ethanol) are fresh.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ebv3","text":"Xylene and Ethanol are very volatile and well as potentially hazardous to inhale. Use a certified fume hood for all steps in this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"a6a14","text":"Hematoxylin, Richard Allen Scientific, Thermo Scientific, catalog #7211","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fpult","text":"Eosin Y, Richard Allen Scientific, Thermo Scientific, catalog # 7111","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ibgj","text":"Clarifier, Richard Allen Scientific, Thermo Scientific, catalog # 7401","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bn4fe","text":"Bluing Reagent, Richard Allen Scientific, Thermo Scientific catalog # 7301","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f6t6d","text":"Cytoseal XYL, Thermo Scientific catalog # 8312-4","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"684vi","text":"Leica Apperio ImageScope Pathology Slide viewing Software","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: De-Paraffization of slides Place slides in to oven at 60 o C for one hour
Place slides in to Fresh Xylene for 2 minutes, repeat
Rehydration of sections 100% Ethanol, 1 minute, repeat
Rinse in running Tap water until clear
Rinse in tap water for 30 seconds
Rinse in Tap water for 30 seconds
Mount slides with 20 x 50 mm Coverslips using Cytoseal XYL, making sure that there are no air bubbles beneath the coverslip. Press the coverslip gently to remove an air bubbles that may have formed.
Let slides dry in a horizontal position in a fume hood for at least 1 hour. Slides can be stored indefinitely at room temperature in a slide box

For illustration, digital storage and possible pathology review slides are scanned on the Leica Aperio slide scanner. The file output is .svs and can be viewed by downloading freely available software from Leica.
creator: John Herndon
